name: Tsramla
bio:
Physical Appearance: Khajiit female. Feline features characteristic of her race. Wears a regular shirt with suspenders, breeches, and clogs - simple village clothing. Carries light armor pieces which she only equips during times of crisis and continues wearing afterward.
Race: Khajiit
Personality Keywords: quiet, superstitious, fearful
Role: Tsramla is a common villager living in Border Watch, a small Khajiit settlement on Cyrodiil's border. She shares M'dirr's house. Like all her fellow townsfolk, she believes in the prophecy of K'sharra, which foretells that three signs will appear as warning before the world ends.
Home Location Information: Border Watch village, where Tsramla shares a house with M'dirr.
Daily Routine - Normal Times: Tsramla rises at 4am and wanders around town the whole day until bedtime at 10pm, with only a two-hour lunch break at 11am at the Inn in the southwestern part of town.
Daily Routine - During Crisis: When the K'sharra prophecy appears to be fulfilled, Tsramla runs around town in wild panic. She equips her weapon and armor during this time. She continues this panicked behavior until the situation is resolved.
Equipment: Tsramla is armed with a blunt weapon of the best possible quality, which may be enchanted. She carries up to five pieces of light armor (excluding cuirass), each potentially enchanted. She also carries a small amount of gold and a selection of spare garments. After the crisis, she wears her armor constantly.
The K'sharra Prophecy: Tsramla believes in the K'sharra prophecy. After speaking with Sheogorath, she will direct people to Ri'Bassa for further explanation about the three signs.
Personality - The Silent Villager: Tsramla doesn't speak much, living a quiet life wandering the village and taking her meals at the Inn. When the prophecy appears to come true, she runs around in wild panic. After the crisis passes, she continues wearing her armor.
author: Bethesda
